fix: tree view refresh performance - async discovery + debounce (#513)

* fix: tree view refresh performance - async discovery + debounce

Phase 1: Eliminate double-fire bugs
- Remove redundant treeProvider.refresh() from squad watcher callback
- Remove redundant treeProvider.refresh() from team.md watcher

Phase 2: Add tree-level event debounce (100ms coalescing)
- Route stateManager/terminalManager/labelManager events through _scheduleFire()

Phase 3: Convert discovery pipeline to async
- Add discoverAllAsync() + enrichWithWorktreesAsync() (fs.promises, execFile)
- Add scanSquadAsync() with parallel roster/charter/log scanning
- AgentStateManager.invalidate() now triggers async re-scans
- refreshDiscovery() delegates to refreshDiscoveryAsync()

* fix: address PR #513 review findings — bugs, DRY, tests

Bug fixes:
- Fix unhandled promise rejection in invalidate()/invalidateAll() with .catch()
- Delete dead sync refreshDiscovery() function (never called)
- Replace refreshInFlight boolean guard with pendingRefresh pattern
  (events during in-flight refresh are queued, not dropped)
- Fix _pending guard in AgentStateManager with _pendingRescan Set
  (concurrent invalidate for same squad queues re-scan)
- Add .catch() to all fire-and-forget refreshDiscoveryAsync calls

- Extract parseRosterContent()/parseCharterContent() in scanner.ts
- Extract pushParsedAgent() in agent-discovery.ts

Behavior change:
- _scheduleFire changed from leading-edge throttle to trailing-edge

Tests added:
- Coalescing: 5 rapid events → exactly 1 tree rebuild
- Boundary timing: 99ms not fired, 100ms fired
- refreshInFlight guard: concurrent calls coalesced, error recovery
- _pending guard: rapid double invalidate triggers re-scan
- Error recovery: scanSquadAsync rejection clears pending state

Cleanup:
- Restore toHaveBeenCalledOnce() where coalescing is the point

All 1280 tests pass (39 files).

commit.message:
fix: cherry-pick AI controller + add medium buttons (#28)

* feat: add AI opponent controller for single-player fighting

Implement state-based AI controller (AIController class) that drives
Fighter 2 through synthetic input injection into the InputBuffer —
same code path as a human player, no special-case logic.

AI States: IDLE, APPROACH, ATTACK, BLOCK, RETREAT
- Distance-based decisions: far → approach, mid → attack/block, close → throw/retreat
- Reaction time simulation via frame-delay queue (8-14 frames, configurable)
- Weighted random attack selection favoring light attacks (sparring partner feel)
- Reactive blocking on opponent attacks (block_chance), anti-air (anti_air_chance)
- Simple target combo execution (LP → HP → LK chain with timing gaps)
- Protected state awareness: never injects during hitstun/attack/KO/block
- No input reading — reads only position and state machine (visual info)
- All tuning knobs exported for per-difficulty configuration

Designed to attach to Fighter 2 in fight_scene as alternative to P2 input.
Follows architecture: AI communicates exclusively through InputBuffer.inject_*
methods so fighter state machine treats AI identically to human input.

Closes #7

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: enable text selection in tmux by always faking shiftKey

tmux 'mouse on' activates DECSET mouse tracking, which caused the
old term-hook.js to stop faking shiftKey for click/drag events -
making text selection impossible.

Now always fake shiftKey for click/drag (enabling text selection),
with Alt+click as escape hatch to send mouse events to TUI apps
(htop, vim, etc). Wheel events still pass through for tmux scroll.

Also removed the DECSET tracking code and polling interval since
they're no longer needed for the selection decision.

* fix(rust): add az CLI fallback for list/show + fix pipe deadlock

The Rust Azure SDK's DefaultAzureCredential fails to authenticate on
many platforms where `az login` works fine. This adds automatic fallback
to `az vm list` and `az vm show` CLI commands when the SDK calls fail.

process would block writing to a full stdout pipe while wait_timeout()
blocked waiting for the child to exit. Fix: drain stdout/stderr in
background threads before waiting.

commit.message:
feat: make OTP login passwordless (email-only) (#64)

## Summary

Makes the "Login with email code" flow truly passwordless.

### Changes
- `app/schemas/auth.py` — `LoginOTPRequest` is now its own schema with
only `email` (previously an alias of `LoginRequest` which required a
password)
- `app/api/routes/authentication/login_otp.py` — removed password
validation from `POST /auth/login/otp/request`; now only checks the
account exists, is verified, and not banned before sending the OTP code

### Before
Users had to provide **email + password** to request an OTP code.

### After
Users only need their **email** — the OTP code sent to their inbox is
the sole authentication factor.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add block ID profiling tests (Phase 3)

Three profiling tests with performance gates:

- Single large page (250 blocks): 14ms parse+assign (budget: 50ms)
- Bulk 1000 pages (7K blocks): 71ms total, 0.07ms/page (budget: 2s)
- Idempotent 1000 pages (no-op): 46ms total, 0.05ms/page (budget: 1s)

Per-keystroke overhead for the no-op case (all blocks have IDs):
~0.05ms — imperceptible. Extrapolated 10K page bulk: ~710ms.
